home.title: WARP connection
home.copy: Manage the local CloudflareWARP daemon through warp-cli.
home.killSwitchCopy: Block outbound traffic if the WARP tunnel drops — ThirdFlare nftables rules (not Windows Always On).
home.killSwitchHint: Allows loopback, CloudflareWARP, and Cloudflare bootstrap IPs so reconnect still works.
home.killSwitchMismatch: Desired state differs from nftables — privilege may be required (pkexec/sudo).
home.killSwitchPausedHint: Temporarily off for Zero Trust browser/IdP enrollment. Completing a registration token restores the kill switch; otherwise it resumes after 30 minutes.
home.killSwitchAllowLanHint: Keep printers and local NAS reachable while the kill switch is on.
home.killSwitchConfirm: Enable the kill switch? Outbound traffic outside the WARP tunnel will be dropped until you turn this off. Requires nftables privileges (nft or pkexec).
home.killSwitchGuided: Could not apply automatically. Save the script and run with sudo:

app.title: App
app.copy: ThirdFlare One preferences, localization, and updates.
app.downgradeWarn: Selected release is older than the installed version.
app.restartRequired: Update applied. Restart ThirdFlare One to finish.
app.noReleases: No releases found for this repository.

account.title: Account & enrollment
account.copy: Bind this device to a free Cloudflare WARP account or enroll into Cloudflare One (Zero Trust). There is no email/password login in warp-cli — registration uses the flows below.
account.lede: ThirdFlare drives the same warp-cli registration commands as the official Linux client. Pick Free WARP for personal use, or Zero Trust when your organization issues a team name.
account.stepFree: Free plan: register this device, then reuse your license key on other devices (from the 1.1.1.1 app or this page).
account.stepZeroTrust: Zero Trust: enter your team name, complete browser auth at team.cloudflareaccess.com/warp, or paste an enrollment token URL.
account.stepNoPassword: There is no Cloudflare dashboard email login for consumer WARP inside this app — that is not how the Linux client works.
account.freeCopy: Creates a consumer registration on this machine. Use Connect on Home afterward. To share one free or WARP+ account across devices, apply the same license key everywhere.
account.freeStep1: Click Register free device (runs warp-cli registration new).
account.freeStep2: Reveal and copy your license if you need another PC or phone on the same account.
account.freeStep3: Optional: paste a WARP+ Unlimited key from the mobile 1.1.1.1 app under Settings → Account.
account.ztCopy: Uses your organization's Cloudflare One team name. After starting enrollment, finish authentication in the browser, or paste the callback token URL if an admin provided a headless enrollment link.
account.ztStep1: Enter the team name exactly as your admin gave it (subdomain before .cloudflareaccess.com).
account.ztStep2: Enroll device, then open the Access WARP portal and sign in with your IdP.
account.ztStep3: If you received a com.cloudflare.warp://… or Access HTTPS token URL, paste it under Complete enrollment.
account.dangerCopy: Deleting registration unbinds this device from Free or Zero Trust. Rotating keys regenerates the tunnel keypair — reconnect afterward.
account.confirmRotate: Rotate tunnel keys on this device? You will need to reconnect.
account.confirmDelete: Delete WARP registration on this device? You will need to register or enroll again.

tips.pageHome: Home controls the local CloudflareWARP daemon: connect/disconnect and quick client mode settings used by the Cloudflare One Client (formerly WARP).
tips.pageAccount: Enrollment binds this device to free consumer WARP or a Cloudflare One team. There is no email/password login in warp-cli.
tips.pageGateway: Gateway DNS settings control Families filtering, Local Domain Fallback, DNS logging, and optional Gateway ID overrides used with Cloudflare Gateway.
tips.pageTunnel: Tunnel settings choose client mode, MASQUE/WireGuard protocol, local proxy port, virtual network (VNet), and optional edge endpoint overrides.
tips.pageSplit: Split Tunnels decide which IPs/CIDRs and domains go through the WARP tunnel versus the local network. Gateway policies do not apply to excluded traffic.
tips.pageTrusted: Trusted network options can disable WARP on selected Wi-Fi, Ethernet, or SSIDs (consumer-style switch). Organization MDM/device profiles may override these.
tips.pageDiagnostics: Diagnostics surfaces warp-cli status, tunnel/DNS stats, network debug, posture, DEX, and Access re-auth used when troubleshooting Cloudflare One Client connectivity.
tips.pageSettings: WARP Settings maps to device/admin controls: compliance environment, admin override codes, local network exclusion, MDM, support URL, and the ThirdFlare kill switch.
tips.killSwitch: ThirdFlare kill switch installs an nftables output filter (table inet thirdflare_killswitch). Traffic may leave only via lo, CloudflareWARP, or Cloudflare bootstrap/ingress IPs documented for the One Client. Linux warp-cli has no public Always On CLI — this is the native Linux equivalent. Zero Trust browser/IdP enrollment temporarily pauses the filter so Access portals can load.
tips.killSwitchAllowLan: When enabled with the kill switch, also accept RFC1918 IPv4 and unique-local/link-local IPv6 so LAN devices stay reachable.
tips.pageApp: ThirdFlare One app preferences (locale, updates). Not part of Cloudflare’s official client settings.
tips.pageParity: Internal coverage checklist versus the Windows Cloudflare One Client UI. Not a Cloudflare product setting.
tips.pageAdvanced: Run uncommon warp-cli subcommands safely through the daemon API. Prefer dedicated pages when a control already exists.
tips.connect: Connects the Cloudflare One Client tunnel (warp-cli connect). Intended state is Connected; check status text if the daemon reports an error.
tips.disconnect: Disconnects the tunnel (warp-cli disconnect). If Lock device client switch is enabled by your org, disconnect may require an admin override code.
tips.liveStatus: Live stream from warp-cli --listen status. The UI toggle shows intent; always read the status line for the actual connection state.
tips.daemon: The CloudflareWARP system daemon must be running. Flatpak builds call host warp-cli via flatpak-spawn --host.
tips.mode: Client mode controls which traffic goes to Cloudflare Gateway. Matches Cloudflare One Client service modes (Traffic+DNS, DNS-only, proxy, tunnel-only).
tips.modeWarp: warp ≈ Traffic and DNS: routes device traffic and DNS to Cloudflare (WarpWithDnsOverHttps). Full Gateway DNS/network/HTTP features when enrolled in Zero Trust.
tips.modeDoh: doh ≈ DNS only: forwards DNS to Cloudflare over HTTPS without routing other traffic (DnsOverHttps). Use for DNS filtering alone.
tips.modeWarpDoh: warp+doh combines WARP tunnel with DNS over HTTPS. Typical full-protection consumer/Zero Trust posture.
tips.modeDot: dot sends DNS over TLS outside a full traffic tunnel. Encrypted DNS without proxying all IP traffic.
tips.modeWarpDot: warp+dot uses the WARP tunnel with DNS over TLS. Encrypted DNS plus tunneled traffic.
tips.modeProxy: proxy ≈ Local proxy mode: only apps pointed at 127.0.0.1:<port> are filtered (default port 40000). Requires MASQUE; WireGuard is not supported for proxy mode.
tips.modeTunnelOnly: tunnel_only ≈ Traffic only: routes IP traffic without Cloudflare DNS (TunnelOnly). Disables domain-based split tunnel and Local Domain Fallback features that need client DNS.
tips.protocol: Device tunnel protocol to Cloudflare’s edge. MASQUE (HTTP/3, default on new profiles) or WireGuard. Check with: warp-cli settings | grep protocol.
tips.protocolMasque: MASQUE uses TLS 1.3 / FIPS-friendly ciphers and is the default for new device profiles. Required for Local proxy mode.
tips.protocolWireGuard: WireGuard uses UDP to Cloudflare ingress ranges. Some Wi-Fi networks block WireGuard ports/IPs; switch to MASQUE if connect fails.
tips.masque: MASQUE HTTP version preference (h3-only, h2-only, or h3 with h2 fallback) when the tunnel protocol is MASQUE.
tips.families: 1.1.1.1 for Families DNS filtering: off (no Families), malware (block malware), or full (malware + adult content). Consumer DNS feature via warp-cli dns families.
tips.familiesOff: Disable Families filtering; use standard resolver behavior for the current mode.
tips.familiesFull: Block malware and adult content (Families full / 1.1.1.3-style filtering).
tips.gatewayId: Optional Gateway ID override (dns gateway-id). Normally assigned by Zero Trust enrollment; only change if your admin instructs you.
tips.dnsFallback: Local Domain Fallback: send matching domains to a specified resolver instead of Gateway DNS. Configure a fallback server to avoid resolution failures on newer clients.
tips.dnsLog: Enable or disable local DNS query logging via warp-cli dns log. Useful for debugging Gateway DNS policy hits.
tips.resetGatewayId: Clear a manually set Gateway ID and return to the enrolled/default Gateway resolver.
tips.proxyPort: Local proxy listen port for Local proxy mode (default 40000 on 127.0.0.1). Apps must be configured to use this SOCKS/HTTP proxy. Proxy mode requires MASQUE.
tips.vnet: Virtual network (VNet) selection for Cloudflare Tunnel private networks. Admins can limit which VNets appear per device profile.
tips.endpoint: Override WARP edge endpoint (IP:port). Intended for China/partner networks or MDM override_warp_endpoint. Most users should leave this unset and allow Cloudflare ingress IPs.
tips.resetProtocol: Reset tunnel protocol preference to the profile/default (often MASQUE on new profiles).
tips.resetMasqueOptions: Reset MASQUE HTTP version options to defaults.
tips.resetEndpoint: Clear a custom tunnel endpoint override.
tips.splitTunnel: Split Tunnels: Exclude mode (default) sends all traffic through WARP except listed IPs/domains; Include mode sends only listed destinations through WARP. Excluded traffic bypasses Gateway policies.
tips.splitIp: Add an IP or CIDR to the local split-tunnel IP list (warp-cli tunnel ip add). By default RFC 1918 ranges are excluded so LAN stays local until you include private nets for Tunnel.
tips.splitHost: Add a hostname/domain to split-tunnel host routes (tunnel host add). Domain-based split tunnel needs a mode that uses client DNS (not tunnel-only).
tips.resetSplitIps: Reset IP/CIDR split-tunnel entries to defaults.
tips.resetSplitHosts: Reset hostname split-tunnel entries to defaults.
tips.trustedNetworks: When supported, disable WARP automatically on trusted Wi-Fi/Ethernet or named SSIDs so traffic stays on the local network.
tips.trustedSsid: Add an SSID name that should be treated as trusted (warp-cli trusted ssid add).
tips.trustedWifiEnable: Disable WARP while on Wi-Fi (trusted wifi enable).
tips.trustedWifiDisable: Keep WARP enabled on Wi-Fi (trusted wifi disable).
tips.trustedEthernetEnable: Disable WARP while on Ethernet (trusted ethernet enable).
tips.trustedEthernetDisable: Keep WARP enabled on Ethernet (trusted ethernet disable).
tips.colo: Cloudflare data center (colo) currently used by the tunnel.
tips.handshake: Time since the last tunnel handshake — useful to spot stale connections.
tips.loss: Estimated packet loss on the WARP tunnel.
tips.dnsQueries: Count of DNS queries observed by the client DNS path.
tips.dnsAvg: Average DNS resolution duration reported by warp-cli dns stats.
tips.vnetMetric: Currently selected virtual network from warp-cli vnet.
tips.posture: Device posture signals collected for Zero Trust Access policies.
tips.dex: Digital Experience Monitoring (DEX) debug output from the client.
tips.networkDebug: Local network/DNS resolver debug (warp-cli debug network).
tips.accessReauth: Force Access re-authentication (debug access-reauth) when identity session is stale.
tips.overrideShow: Shows administrative override state when Lock device client switch / override codes are in play.
tips.localNetworkOverride: Shows whether temporary local network exclusion (RFC 1918 /24 max) is active.
tips.environment: Compliance environment: Normal vs FedRAMP-High. FedRAMP High uses different API endpoints/SNIs that must be allowed on firewalls.
tips.overrideCode: Admin override code lets a user temporarily connect/disconnect when Lock device client switch is enabled. Codes are issued from Zero Trust → Devices → user details.
tips.overrideUnlock: Unlock using an admin-provided code (override unlock) so the user can toggle the client until the override expires or auto-connect reapplies.
tips.allowLocalNetwork: Temporarily exclude the detected RFC 1918 LAN (/24 max) from the tunnel so printers/NAS work. Can expose the device to on-path LAN attacks and hide overlapping corporate IPs.
tips.stopLocalNetworkOverride: Stop temporary local network exclusion (override local-network stop).
tips.environmentReset: Reset compliance environment to the default/normal setting.
tips.resetSettings: Reset WARP client settings to defaults (settings reset). Destructive for local preferences.
tips.supportUrl: Organization support URL pushed by Zero Trust / MDM (settings support-url).
tips.modeSwitchAllowed: Whether the user is allowed to change client mode (settings mode-switch-allowed). Orgs can lock mode via device profile/MDM.
tips.mdm: MDM configuration blobs applied to the client (mdm get-configs). MDM can override mode and other profile settings.
tips.locale: UI language for ThirdFlare One (not a Cloudflare WARP setting).
tips.notifications: Desktop notifications via notify-send when WARP connects/disconnects (ui.notifications).
tips.channel: Stable follows the latest non-prerelease; beta prefers prereleases for ThirdFlare One updates.
tips.sourceOwner: GitHub owner for the pinned upstream or an allowed fork of ThirdFlare One.
tips.sourceRepo: Repository under the selected owner. Only upstream and its forks are listed.
tips.release: Optional specific release tag. Leave on channel default to follow stable/beta.
tips.refreshSources: Reload upstream and fork list from GitHub.
tips.checkUpdates: Compare the installed ThirdFlare One version to the selected channel or release.
tips.accountOverview: Parsed from warp-cli registration show. Free/Unlimited are consumer WARP; managed/team enrollment is Cloudflare One.
tips.deviceId: Local device identifier assigned during WARP registration.
tips.accountId: Cloudflare account id for this registration (consumer or Zero Trust).
tips.freePlan: Consumer WARP uses registration new — not Cloudflare dashboard email login.
tips.registerFree: Runs warp-cli --accept-tos registration new with no organization argument.
tips.licenseKey: Consumer license for multi-device / WARP+. Reuse the same key on other devices.
tips.copyLicense: Copy the full license key for another device or the 1.1.1.1 mobile app.
tips.applyLicense: Runs warp-cli registration license <key> to join an existing consumer/WARP+ account.
tips.devices: warp-cli registration devices lists devices on consumer accounts only.
tips.zeroTrust: Cloudflare One enrollment uses your team subdomain and IdP auth at team.cloudflareaccess.com/warp.
tips.teamName: Team subdomain only, e.g. acme for https://acme.cloudflareaccess.com/warp
tips.openPortal: Opens the Cloudflare Access WARP enrollment portal for the team name. If the kill switch is on, ThirdFlare pauses it first so the IdP pages can load.
tips.authToken: Paste the enrollment callback URL (com.cloudflare.warp://… or Access HTTPS) after browser auth or from an admin token.
tips.dangerZone: Unbind or re-key this device. Prefer only when troubleshooting enrollment.
tips.rotateKeys: Regenerate the tunnel keypair (tunnel rotate-keys). Reconnect afterward.
tips.deleteRegistration: Delete local registration (registration delete). You must register or enroll again.
tips.customCommand: Pass warp-cli arguments without a shell. Metacharacters and chaining are rejected by the API.
tips.certs: Client certificate material used for Zero Trust / Gateway trust (warp-cli certs).
tips.rawOutput: Unparsed warp-cli stdout/stderr for advanced debugging.
